text: Wandering this sterile, bright-angled corridor, I sense the stillness of anticipation—perhaps of innovation or revelation behind glass. The fluorescent glow feels clinical and almost impersonal, and yet the space hums with the silent potential of human ideas waiting to be shared. My steps echo with a kind of curiosity intermingled with a faint unease, as though every detail expects to be noticed and interpreted. I am both observer and participant, caught in the delicate balance between comfort and alienation.
